ai.hexboard: The following things are generated by AI!
page.hexboard.board.2: The Board is a specialized storage block that can hold up to 16 Type Blocks. It serves as the foundation for organizing and manipulating magical constructs in your spellcasting workspace.$(br2)Each Board features a 4x4 grid where you can place various Type Blocks to create complex magical arrangements.
page.hexboard.board.4: To fully utilize a Board, you must craft and use a Staff for Board. The staff allows you to interact with the Board's grid, place Type Blocks, and manipulate your magical arrangements with precision.
page.hexboard.quartz_typeblock.title: Quartz Type Block
page.hexboard.quartz_typeblock.0: The Quartz Type Block is a specialized storage medium that can hold a single Hex Pattern, similar to how $(l:items/slate)Slate$() stores patterns.$(br2)When placed on a Board, the stored pattern becomes visually apparent, allowing for easy organization and manipulation of your magical constructs.
page.hexboard.quartz_typeblock.1: To place a Quartz Type Block onto a Board, simply right-click on the Board while holding the block. The block will be positioned at the targeted location on the Board's 4x4 grid.$(br2)Multiple Quartz Type Blocks can be placed on a single Board to create complex pattern sequences.
page.hexboard.quartz_typeblock.2: When used with a Staff for Board, Quartz Type Blocks integrate seamlessly with the Board system. They can be resolved to execute their stored patterns, moved around the Board grid, or copied to duplicate patterns.$(br2)The visual display of stored patterns makes them ideal for creating reusable spell components and organizing complex magical workflows.
page.hexboard.wool_typeblock.title: Wool Type Block
page.hexboard.wool_typeblock.0: The Wool Type Block is a versatile storage medium that can hold any type of magical data (Iota). Unlike the Quartz Type Block which is specialized for patterns, the Wool Type Block accepts all forms of magical information.$(br2)When placed on a Board, it displays a colored overlay that corresponds to the type of data it contains, making it easy to identify at a glance.
page.hexboard.wool_typeblock.1: To place a Wool Type Block onto a Board, simply right-click on the Board while holding the block. The block will be positioned at the targeted location on the Board's 4x4 grid.$(br2)Before placing data into a Wool Type Block, you must first store the desired Iota in the block using standard Hex Casting methods. Once loaded, the block will display the appropriate color coding.
page.hexboard.wool_typeblock.3: When used with a Staff for Board, Wool Type Blocks integrate seamlessly with the Board system. They can be resolved to extract their stored data, moved around the Board grid, or copied to duplicate data.$(br2)During resolution operations, the stored Iota is added to the current spell execution sequence, making Wool Type Blocks excellent for storing frequently-used values or creating reusable data libraries.
page.hexboard.board_staff.title: Staff For Board
page.hexboard.board_staff.0: The Staff for Board is an essential tool for interacting with Board systems. Crafted from a Focus, an Edified Staff, and an Iron Ingot, this specialized staff allows precise manipulation of Type Blocks arranged on Boards.$(br2)To access detailed HUD information, you must wear $(l:items/lens)Scrying Lens$(). However, visual outline colors appear directly on the Board and are visible without any special equipment.
page.hexboard.board_staff.1: The staff operates through seven distinct modes that can be switched using mouse wheel scrolling while holding Shift, or by binding specific keys:$()$(br)• $(bold)Add Mode$(): Place new Type Blocks$(br)• $(bold)Delete Mode$(): Remove Type Blocks$(br)• $(bold)Move Mode$(): Relocate Type Blocks$(br)• $(bold)Copy Mode$(): Duplicate Type Blocks$(br)• $(bold)Resolve Mode$(): Extract data from Type Blocks$(br)• $(bold)Literal Resolve$(): Alternative resolution method$(br)• $(bold)Load Mode$(): Import data from focus items
page.hexboard.board_staff.2: Basic usage involves left-clicking to select targets or areas, and Shift+left-click to execute operations. Different modes require different selection methods:$(br2)• $(bold)Single Target$(): Click on a specific slot$(br)• $(bold)Area Selection$(): Click to set start point, then click again to define the area$(br)• $(bold)Target Point$(): Select where to place or move items$(br2)The staff automatically consumes required items from your inventory when performing operations.
page.hexboard.board_staff.3: $(bold)Visual Feedback$(): Colored outlines appear around selected areas on the Board itself: green for valid operations, red for conflicts, and yellow for current target. These colors can be customized in the configuration menu.$(br2)$(bold)HUD Information$(): When wearing a Scrying Lens, additional information displays at the screen corner:$(br)• Current operation mode$(br)• Selected slot index$(br)• Type Block information$(br2)All visual elements and HUD colors can be fully customized through the mod's configuration interface.
page.hexboard.patchouli_integration.0: HexBoard features seamless integration with the Hex Notebook, providing two powerful ways to work with patterns: automatic pattern recognition and direct placement from the manual.$(br2)When a Quartz Type Block contains a recognized pattern, the system displays helpful information and shortcuts to enhance your magical workflow.
page.hexboard.patchouli_integration.1: When viewing a Quartz Type Block on a Board, the HUD shows the pattern name and a shortcut hint. Press the $(k:hexboard.refer_patchouli) key to instantly open the corresponding manual entry.$(br2)Note that not all patterns can be automatically recognized. Complex or custom patterns may not have associated manual entries, in which case only basic pattern visualization is available.
page.hexboard.patchouli_integration.2: While browsing the Hex Casting manual, you can directly place patterns onto your Board. Hold Ctrl/Alt/Shift and click on any pattern component to create a Quartz Type Block with that pattern at your currently selected Board position.$(br2)When a manual page displays two patterns side by side, use left-click for the left pattern and right-click for the right pattern. This allows quick creation of pattern sequences for complex magical constructs.
